tweets.text:
The Secret Service will add the name of former Special Agent in Charge Michael Rice to its Wall of Honor this week.

SAIC Rice passed away in 2019 from cancer linked to his work at the Fresh Kills New York landfill site following the September 11 attacks. He will be the 41st employee added to the Wall, and his name will also be inscribed on the National Law Enforcement Officers Memorial in Washington, D.C.

Read more about this incredible hero at https://t.co/KHKd5lj6a9

#SecretService #WallOfHonor #NeverForgotten
tweets.text_resolved:
The Secret Service will add the name of former Special Agent in Charge Michael Rice to its Wall of Honor this week.

SAIC Rice passed away in 2019 from cancer linked to his work at the Fresh Kills New York landfill site following the September 11 attacks. He will be the 41st employee added to the Wall, and his name will also be inscribed on the National Law Enforcement Officers Memorial in Washington, D.C.

Read more about this incredible hero at https://www.secretservice.gov/newsroom/behind-the-shades/2026/05/us-secret-service-adds-former-special-agent-charge-michael-rice

#SecretService #WallOfHonor #NeverForgotten

author.description: The Secret Service is mandated by Congress to carry out an integrated mission of protection and criminal investigations.

tweets.text:
Special Agent in Charge Michael Rice was a Vietnam War veteran, a 9/11 recovery volunteer and had a 26-year Secret Service career. He embodied the quiet professionalism and selfless service this agency was built on. His name is now permanently inscribed on the Secret Service Wall of Honor and the National Law Enforcement Officers Memorial.

We are grateful for his service. We will not forget him.

https://t.co/KHKd5lj6a9

#SecretService #WallOfHonor #NeverForgotten

tweets.text:
Keeping kids safe is a team effort.

Operation Safe Kids (OSK) gives parents a photo, biographical info and inkless fingerprints of their child — all stored with you, not us. If your child is ever reported missing, that information is ready to help bring them home.

Request a free OSK event in your area: https://t.co/sDg58dkWlH

#OperationSafeKids #USSChildSafetyMonth

tweets.text:
Ever wonder what it's like to work for the Secret Service? Bring your questions and join us virtually May 13 at 6:00 PM ET!

We are hosting a virtual town hall where you can hear directly from active Special Agents, Secret Service Police Officers, Special Operations Division team members and recruiting staff about the hiring process and what working for the Secret Service is like.

Seats are limited for this exclusive event, so register now at https://t.co/ulCialWBio

#SecretService #WeAreHiring

tweets.text:
The internet can be a dangerous place for kids. The Secret Service can help.

The Childhood Smart Program (CSP) brings free, age-appropriate safety presentations to classrooms, camps and community events. These topical presentations cover online safety, cyberbullying and abduction prevention.

Request a free presentation in your area: https://t.co/sDg58dkWlH

#ChildhoodSmart #InternetSafety #ChildSafety

tweets.text:
The Secret Service is hiring Unmanned Aircraft Systems Operators for our Office of Technical Operations, Aviation and Airspace Security Division.

In this role, you'll develop airspace security plans and lead real-time aerial surveillance in support of both protective operations and criminal investigations, two of the most demanding missions in Federal law enforcement.

Apply now -- this opportunity flies away May 25!

https://t.co/Mz3BNGwRX6

tweets.text:
The Secret Service and @NCMEC are teaming up to protect kids and bringing resources directly to your community.

From inkless fingerprinting for parents to internet safety presentations for K-12 students, our child safety programs are free and available for schools and community events nationwide.

✉️ Request one today: FSDNCMEC@USSS.DHS.GOV

#USSSChildSafetyMonth #InternetSafety #NCMEC

tweets.text:
Tomorrow, the window closes on one of the most unique jobs in Federal law enforcement.

The Secret Service is hiring Unmanned Aircraft Systems Operators to conduct aerial surveillance, develop flight operations plans and evaluate emerging UAS technologies in support of mission-critical protective operations. You'll be a vital part of the team protecting Presidents and dignitaries, and also assisting with criminal investigations.

It's a challenging job, but the opportunity closes tomorrow!

https://t.co/Mz3BNGwRX6
